business.headline: Delhi govt cuts VAT on jet fuel; move to benefit airlines, passengersDelhi has cut VAT on aviation turbine fuel from 25 per cent to 7 per cent after Maharashtra made a similar move. The step is aimed at easing pressure on airlines and passengers as global jet fuel prices climb.

Govt restricts silver bar imports, 99.9% purity category now needs permitThe Centre has restricted imports of certain silver bars, including 99.9% purity silver, with immediate effect amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

Selective compliance, systemic exclusion: How workers are left outIn the seventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, a factory inspection from 1988 reveals how workers, though recorded for contributions, remained excluded from statutory benefits through selective and incomplete compliance.

No Claim Bonus: The motor insurance reward many drivers lose unknowinglyHidden inside every motor insurance policy is something called No Claim Bonus, or NCB, a reward insurers give customers for not making claims. Over time, it can reduce premiums significantly. Yet despite being one of the most valuable benefits in motor insurance, it is also one of the least understood.

Inflation finds new fuelHandcrafted ByOur Editors India's latest fuel price hike could trigger a wider inflation ripple across the economy, raising concerns over costlier food, deliveries, transport and growing pressure on household budgets.

Govt hikes export duty on petrol to Rs 3 per litre, cuts diesel, ATF levies from May 16The differential taxation strategy is expected to boost government revenue from petroleum exports without affecting price stability for Indian consumers.

Fuel price hike right move, oil firms can't absorb loss for long period: Ashok LahiriIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Dr Ashok Lahiri, the newly appointed Vice-Chairman of NITI Aayog, discussed the recent fuel price hikes, the state of the Indian economy, and the impact of the West Asia conflict.
Sensex, Nifty slip as rupee hits record lowThe benchmark BSE Sensex fell 160.73 points, or 0.21%, to close at 75,237.99, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 46.10 points, or 0.19%, to settle at 23,643.50.

Reliance-Disney's JioStar sues Zee over alleged unauthorised film broadcasts: ReportJioStar has moved against Zee over alleged unauthorised telecasts of 12 Bollywood films. The clash adds to a widening rights battle between two major media companies.

Maharashtra cuts VAT on jet fuel from 18% to 7% amid rising aviation costsFuel is one of the biggest expenses for airlines and accounts for nearly 35-40% of an airline's operating costs. The tax reduction is expected to lower refuelling costs at Mumbai airport, which is India's second-busiest airport after Delhi and handles around 15% of the country's air traffic.

Petrol, diesel prices could rise over next 3-4 months amid crude oil crisisThe Rs 3-per-litre fuel price hike may not be the last, with analysts warning that persistently high crude oil prices could trigger more petrol and diesel price increases over coming three to four months.
Rupee hits all-time low, breaches 96 for 1st time against US dollarThe rupee weakened 0.3% to 96.05 against the dollar, crossing its previous all-time low of 95.9575 touched in the previous session.

HPCL dealers allege pressure to push 'Power Petrol' amid fuel price hikePetrol dealers have alleged that state-run oil firms are pressuring them to prioritise premium fuel orders amid a fresh Rs 3 per litre hike. The claim has added to concerns over consumer choice and retail fuel availability during the West Asia-linked energy crisis.

Beyond the petrol pump: How higher fuel prices could hit India's economyThe Rs 3-per-litre petrol and diesel price hike may only be the start of a new cycle of fuel price increases as crude oil prices remain high, with economists warning of wider pressure on inflation, growth, household budgets and the rupee

Petrol, diesel prices hiked after years. Here's what else could get costlierRising petrol and diesel prices are starting to push up transport and household costs across India. Economists say the impact could spread to food, deliveries, air travel and inflation if crude stays high.
Sensex, Nifty open higher despite rise in fuel prices; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 292.55 points to 75,691.27, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 83.70 points to 23,773.30 as of 9:34 am.

Fuel costlier by Rs 3 per litre: Why government raised petrol, diesel pricesThe government and state-run oil companies raised petrol and diesel prices by Rs 3 per litre on Friday after keeping rates unchanged for weeks.

8th Pay Commission: What happened at the Cabinet Secretary-led NCJCM meetingAt the NCJCM meeting on May 11, employee unions placed pay, pension, healthcare and staffing demands before senior government officials. The discussion gained added weight as it ran alongside 8th Pay Commission consultations and prompted directions on several pending issues.

India's fuel crisis could push a long-overdue rethink of how cities movePM Modi's seven appeals are the right signal at the right time. But fuel security and clean air require infrastructure, not just intention, and that demands a place in every state and central budget.

Petrol, diesel prices hiked by Rs 3 nationwide; CNG also up in Delhi by Rs 2 Live UpdatesIndia had so far avoided a major increase in retail fuel prices despite surging global crude oil prices as a result of the West Asian conflict. However, speculation over another round of hikes had been building for days as global crude continued to climb.

entertainment.headline: Aditi Rao Hydari stuns in Chaarbagh-inspired champagne silk saree at Cannes Actor Aditi Rao Hydari made a striking Cannes 2026 appearance in a champagne silk tissue saree inspired by Chaarbagh. Her latest Instagram post has now caught fans' attention.

Mouni Roy, Suraj Nambiar reconnect on Instagram days after announcing separation Actor Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar's Instagram activity sparked fresh discussion on Saturday after the estranged couple started following each other again following their recent separation announcement. Fans are now closely tracking their online activity.

Kamal Haasan meets CM Vijay, raises six key demands of the Tamil film industry Actor-politician Kamal Haasan met Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay and raised six key demands linked to the Tamil film industry. The veteran actor also praised the new government's recent decisions.

The Wonderfools review: Park Eun-bin powers this chaotic yet fun superhero comedyRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Netflix's The Wonderfools follows small-town misfits who stumble into superpowers amid Y2K panic. Park Eun-bin's assured performance gives the show's chaos enough heart to outweigh its uneven pacing.

Won't act until my divorce comes through: Tamil actor Ravi Mohan breaks down Tamil actor Ravi Mohan turned emotional during a press meet in Chennai as he spoke about his divorce battle with estranged wife Aarti, the cyberbullying he has faced and his relationship with alleged partner Keneeshaa Francis.

Vijay Deverakonda congratulates Suriya after Karuppu opens to overwhelming response Vijay Deverakonda reacted to the strong response for Suriya's Karuppu and its Telugu version Veerabhadrudu. Here's what the actor said as the fantasy thriller continues to gain momentum at the box office

Berlin and the Lady with an Ermine review: Pedro Alonso returns with flairRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Returning to the Money Heist universe, Netflix's Berlin and the Lady with an Ermine delivers a lighter, more playful caper that relies heavily on Pedro Alonso's undeniable charm and flair.

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 trends at No 1 in Pakistan after Netflix release A content creator has claimed Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge reached number one on Netflix in Pakistan after its international release on May 14.

Vijay, Rajinikanth, MGR: India's love affair with celebrity worship Why does India turn its film stars into gods? From women writing letters in blood for Rajesh Khanna to temples built for Rajinikanth and Vijay, this is the story of how celebrity worship in India blurred the line between fandom, faith and political power.

Trisha Krishnan blushes as fan asks about CM Vijay at Karuppu screening. Video At the Karuppu screening, actor Trisha Krishnan was left blushing as a fan asked her about Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay.

Salman Khan on his film selection process: Never read a script in my entire life Salman Khan has said that he has never read a script in his entire life. He was recently seen in a cameo appearance in Riteish Deshmukh's Marathi film Raja Shivaji.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do box office Day 1: Ayushmann's sequel behind Kartik's film Ayushmann Khurrana's Pati Patni Aur Woh Do released on May 15 had a modest opening. The film witnessed mixed reviews, low occupancy and a lower start than Kartik Aaryan's 2019 film, Pati Patni Aur Woh.

Kamal Haasan calls for production cuts in Indian cinema amid West Asia crisis Kamal Haasan has asked the Indian film industry to rein in wasteful spending and rethink costly production habits amid rising economic pressure during the West Asia conflict. He said any cost correction must protect workers and push the industry towards more disciplined, sustainable filmmaking.

Kanye West's India debut show cancelled for 2nd time after government directive Kanye West's India debut concert in New Delhi on May 23, 2026, has been cancelled after official security directives. Organisers have promised full refunds and are discussing a fresh date and venue with Kanye's team.

Saif Ali Khan: If you look up Bollywood, you'll see Shah Rukh Khan Saif Ali Khan spoke about Shah Rukh Khan's drive, self-belief and behind-the-scenes rigour, adding that the qualities gave the superstar his enduring stature. Saif's new film, Kartavya, is produced by SRK's banner.

Celebrity hairstylist Aalim Hakim sets rule against stars with ill-mannered managers Celebrity hairstylist Aalim Hakim has announced a major professional decision involving actors and their teams. His statement has sparked fresh conversation about respect behind the scenes in the film industry.

Hokum review: Adam Scott's film is creepy, clever… then completely lostRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Hokum review: Adam Scott plays a troubled horror writer who reaches a remote Irish hotel to scatter his parents' ashes, and becomes fixated on a locked honeymoon suite. Damian McCarthy's film sustains an eerie folklore mood and strong performances, but the narrative grows increasingly uneven.

Shehnaaz Gill dating cricketer Devdutt Padikkal? IPL appearances spark buzz Shehnaaz Gill's repeated appearances at IPL matches featuring Royal Challengers Bengaluru, and her remarks about favourite players, have sparked dating rumours linking her to cricketer Devdutt Padikkal.

general news.headline: Union Minister's son surrenders after look-out notice in sexual assault caseUnion Minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar's son Bandi Bhageerath surrendered before Cyberabad police in a POCSO case after a look-out circular was issued against him, while the Telangana High Court refused interim protection from arrest.

CBI arrests 'mastermind' Pune Botany teacher in NEET-UG paper leak caseThe arrest was made on the basis of questioning of alleged kingpin PV Kulkarni, a retired Chemistry professor, and other accused in the paper leak case, officials said.

CBSE admits 'possibility of errors', opens Class 12 re-checking window from May 19CBSE has opened the Class 12 re-evaluation and verification process for 2026, allowing students to access answer sheets, report errors, and apply for re-checking. CBSE Exam Controller Sanyam Bhardwaj said errors can occur but will be corrected after expert review, even if marks increase or decrease after evaluation.

'Jihadi drug' meant for Gulf seized in India for 1st time. What is it?The NCB seized Captagon tablets worth Rs 182 crore from Gujarat and Delhi under Operation Ragepill. The first such bust in India has raised security concerns as Captagon, known as jihadi drug, has been traced to conflict and war zones in the Middle East.

What a police station reveals about the Indian stateAn Indian police station is where citizens bring missing persons, assaults, thefts and disputes to the state. The scene shows governance working through discretion, overload, negotiation and emotional strain.

Woman paraded semi-nude, garlanded with slippers over temple entry disputeA woman in Maharashtra's Ulhasnagar was allegedly paraded semi-nude with a slipper garland after a dispute over temple entry, with videos of the assault going viral on social media.

CBSE makes 3 languages compulsory in Class 9 from July 1 for 2026-27 batchCBSE has made three languages compulsory for Class 9 students of the 2026-27 academic year from July 1, 2026, as per a latest notification. Students must study at least two Indian languages. Class 10 students will not need to appear for R3 in their board exams. The board has also introduced a transition plan, textbook support, and flexible teaching arrangements for schools implementing the change.

Gujarat man lets landlord rape wife, daughter to settle Rs 2,000 rent debtThe investigation led to the immediate arrest of the labourer and the landlord. While the worker was sent to jail, the landlord has been remanded to one-day police custody.

Won't act until my divorce comes through: Tamil actor Ravi Mohan breaks downTamil actor Ravi Mohan turned emotional during a press meet in Chennai as he spoke about his divorce battle with estranged wife Aarti, the cyberbullying he has faced and his relationship with alleged partner Keneeshaa Francis.

Taps stolen from Congress office in Chhattisgarh, thieves leave 'love you' noteA fresh theft at the Congress district office Rajiv Bhavan in Gandhi Chowk area of Chhattisgarh's Ambikapur raised serious questions by the party over law and order in the city.

Missing Saraswati idol, Ayodhya verdict: Bhojshala ruling raises bigger questionsThe Madhya Pradesh High Court's Bhojshala verdict has done more than settle a long-running dispute in Dhar. By relying heavily on principles laid down in the Ayodhya case, the ruling has reopened larger questions around faith, archaeology, historical claims and the Places of Worship Act.

Why Xi took Trump on a rare tour to Zhongnanhai, China's most secretive power hubChinese President Xi Jinping took US President Donald Trump inside Zhongnanhai, China's tightly guarded Communist Party leadership compound, after talks on Taiwan, trade, Iran and Indo-Pacific tensions. The Zhongnanhai trip was significant against the backdrop of Trump's first China visit in nearly nine years.

Mouni Roy, Suraj Nambiar reconnect on Instagram days after announcing separationActor Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar's Instagram activity sparked fresh discussion on Saturday after the estranged couple started following each other again following their recent separation announcement. Fans are now closely tracking their online activity.

CM Vijay allocates portfolios, keeps Home; Sengottaiyan gets FinanceThe Tamil Nadu government on Saturday allocated portfolios in Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay's Cabinet, with Vijay retaining key departments including Home, Police and Women and Child Welfare, while senior leader KA Sengottaiyan got Finance and youngest minister Selvi S Keerthana was given the Industries portfolio.

Govt restricts silver bar imports, 99.9% purity category now needs permitThe Centre has restricted imports of certain silver bars, including 99.9% purity silver, with immediate effect amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

NEET and CLEAN: Paper leak is not a bug, it's the featurePaper leaks will keep happening in India. That's because parents are forced to pay the current market rate for a future their child deserves, and the system is incorrigible.

The real NEET crisis? Parents who believe any price is worth an MBBS seatHandcrafted ByOur Editors The NEET scandal has exposed more than just leaked papers and broken exam systems. It has revealed the terrifying desperation inside India's middle-class homes, where becoming a doctor is no longer a dream, but a family survival project.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Sack Dharmendra Pradhan now: Rahul Gandhi slams PM Modi over NEET paper leakLeader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi intensified his attack on the Modi government over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak, demanding the immediate dismissal of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

Delhi govt cuts VAT on jet fuel; move to benefit airlines, passengersDelhi has cut VAT on aviation turbine fuel from 25 per cent to 7 per cent after Maharashtra made a similar move. The step is aimed at easing pressure on airlines and passengers as global jet fuel prices climb.

Why India needs Barda to save Asiatic lions as Gir hits capacityIn 2018, a single virus killed 28 Asiatic lions in Gir in weeks, and every wild Asiatic lion on Earth still lives in that one forest. Here is why India is racing to turn Barda Wildlife Sanctuary into an extinction shield before it is too late.

Before lecturing others...: BJP slams Rahul Gandhi for 'politicising' NEET issueThe BJP hit back at Rahul Gandhi after he criticised the government over the NEET-UG 2026 controversy. The exchange deepened the political row as the Centre defended its response and announced further exam reforms.

Fake cop, Tinder app: How honey trap gang extorted men with raids in DelhiDelhi Police has arrested four men for allegedly using dating apps to lure victims into extortion traps. The case has prompted a fresh warning against meeting strangers at isolated locations.

Totally baseless, did not criticise youth: CJI on 'jobless cockroaches' remarkChief Justice of India Surya Kant clarified that his "cockroaches" remark was wrongly projected as criticism of the youth, saying he had actually targeted people using fake degrees to enter professions like law and media.

sports.headline: Litton Das century rescues Bangladesh after Pakistan pace attack burst Litton Das struck a brilliant counterattacking century as Bangladesh recovered from early trouble to post 278 in the first innings of the second Test against Pakistan. The visitors, however, still ended Day 1 in control after a disciplined bowling performance.

Sunil Narine, Finn Allen complicate Gujarat Titans' playoff chances Gujarat Titans came to Kolkata hoping to make it to the playoffs against 8th placed KKR. Instead, they fumbled multiple catches and ended up losing the game by 30 runs.

Manchester City beat Chelsea 1-0 to lift 8th FA Cup amid Premier League push Manchester City beat Chelsea 1-0 in the FA Cup final, with Antoine Semenyo scoring the decisive goal. The win secured a cup double and extended Chelsea's run of final defeats.

13-match wait, Rs 18 crore Pathirana gets injured after bowling 8 balls IPL 2026: Matheesha Pathirana finally debuted for KKR against Gujarat Titans after a long wait. Returning after 13 games, Pathirana bowled a grand total of 8 balls.

Welcome to the IPL press conference: Must-win game, 12 journalists, and a physio Punjab Kings sent head physio Andrew Leipus to address the media before a must-win match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru in Dharamsala. The move intensified questions over accountability, access and how the franchise is handling scrutiny during a six-match losing streak.

Robert Lewandowski leaves Barcelona after instilling champion mentality Legendary Robert Lewandowski is set to leave FC Barcelona after completing a 4-year tenure. Lewandowski arrived at Barcelona when the club was enduring a difficult time and leaves after winning 7 titles.

Canada strippers, pole dancers set to boycott busy F1 weekend. Here's why? Montreal dancers are planning a strike on the eve of the Canadian Grand Prix weekend. The action is meant to hit club owners at their richest moment and sharpen pressure over labour rights.

Virat Kohli praises women's cricket growth, recalls emotional RCB title triumph IPL 2026: Virat Kohli used the RCB Podcast to reflect on women's cricket, T20 change and RCB's maiden IPL title. The discussion showed a player measuring his career increasingly by influence rather than numbers.

Vinesh Phogat lashes out at govt after 21-year-old drowns in swimming pool A 21-year-old in Haryana drowned inside a swimming pool at the Eklavya Stadium in Jind on Thursday, May 14. Wrestler Vinesh Phogat slammed the government over continued deaths of athletes due to systematic failures.

Arshdeep Singh unbothered by social media criticism: Punjab Kings management Andrew Leipus said Arshdeep Singh has stayed calm despite the online reaction to his Tilak Varma remarks. The Punjab Kings camp is treating the noise as peripheral ahead of a crucial match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

Chasing next Chak De moment: Marijne eyes big changes in Indian women's hockeySjoerd Marijne has returned to rebuild India's women's hockey side ahead of a defining run of tournaments. He believes a younger squad, bigger support staff and tougher habits can revive belief that another Chak De moment is possible for the women's team.

Rahul Dravid disagrees with Gambhir's anti-superstar stance: We need heroesRahul Dravid has pushed back against Gautam Gambhir's call to end superstar culture in Indian cricket, arguing that every sport needs heroes.

Man charged after Scottish referee put under police watch for Celtic penalty rowPolice charged a 19-year-old after referee John Beaton's personal details were shared online. The Scottish FA said the case reflects a wider climate of abuse and intimidation around match officials.

ICC meeting in Ahmedabad during IPL final. Will Pak chief Mohsin Naqvi attend?Following the relocation of the ICC meetings to Ahmedabad, PCB Chairman Mohsin Naqvi's travel to India remains highly uncertain despite reports claiming he has been invited to the IPL 2026 final.

Tired of being chased by cameras, Virat Kohli hits out at IPL content cultureVirat Kohli has criticised the IPL's growing content culture, saying constant filming disrupts training and private conversations. His remarks underline the tension between fan engagement demands and a player's need for space.

Thailand Open: Satwik-Chirag complete comeback win to enter 1st final of seasonThailand Open: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty completed a sensational comeback win over Malaysia's Goh Sze Fei and Nur Izzudin to make it to their first final of the season. The Indian star duo won a three-game thriller to make it to the summit clash of the Thailand Open on Sunday, May 16

KKR vs GT: Will Gujarat, rain perform last rites on KKR's slim playoff hopes?KKR have their playoff hopes hanging by a thread and take on an in-form GT side, aiming to secure their spot in the playoffs. Will Gujarat and potential rain spell end for Kolkata's qualification hopes?

Hockey India removes U-18 team coach over misconduct allegationsHockey India has expelled sub-junior support staff member Sudhir Golla from the national under-18 camp following serious allegations of sexual misconduct and negligence during his training stint in Ranchi.

Kamboj was terrified: Ex-IND captain points out when CSK star lost plot vs LSGKris Srikkanth said Anshul Kamboj lost control after Mitchell Marsh attacked him early against LSG. He argued CSK misread the moment and should have persisted with their left-arm quicks, Spencer Johnson and Mukesh Choudhary.

Brand Beckham soars: David Beckham becomes UK's first billionaire sportsmanDavid and Victoria Beckham have crossed £1.185 billion on the latest Sunday Times Rich List. The milestone reflects Inter Miami's surge, Victoria's turnaround and Beckham's enduring commercial pull.

They funded their own dreams. Now Netherlands women are ready to play a World CupThe Netherlands are all set to make their Women's T20 World Cup debut next month in England and Wales. In an exclusive conversation with IndiaToday.in, Dutch skipper Babette de Leede reflected on the team's remarkable journey, from once struggling for recognition and resources to now stepping onto cricket's biggest stage.

Pakistan's Fatima Sana scores fastest Women's T20I fifty, smashes Richa's recordFatima Sana smashed a 15-ball fifty as Pakistan piled up 223 for 4 against Zimbabwe in Karachi. The innings broke the Women's T20I record previously held by Richa Ghosh, Sophie Devine and Phoebe Litchfield.

MS Dhoni set for CSK return vs SunRisers in Chennai? Ex-teammate drops big hintIPL 2026: Ravichandran Ashwin has hinted that MS Dhoni could return for Chennai Super Kings against SunRisers Hyderabad in Chennai. The Super Kings need to win their last two games to retain their hopes of moving into the playoffs.

Ain't trending no more: Akash's note celebration vs CSK leaves Steyn fed upAkash Singh flashed a handwritten note after striking against CSK in Lucknow. But Dale Steyn wasn't too pleased with the celebration, claiming that flashing the note was never a trend in the first place.

Not Dhoni's pragmatism: Ruturaj stuns fans with bold take on CSK's playoff chancesIPL 2026, LSG vs CSK: As a crushing defeat to Lucknow left Chennai's playoff hopes on a knife-edge, captain Ruturaj Gaikwad's surprisingly relaxed assessment of a complex qualification path has left the franchise's faithful thoroughly bewildered.

8 sixes in 16 balls: Lack of clarity gets the better of CSK against LucknowChennai Super Kings have put themselves in a precarious position after losing to bottom-placed Lucknow Super Giants. What led to the loss, which now threatens to put them out of the playoffs race? We take a look at the IPL Play of the Day.

CSK qualification scenario: Can Chennai reach playoffs after loss vs LSG?CSK playoffs qualification scenario: Chennai Super Kings' loss to Lucknow Super Giants meant that the team took a huge blow to their playoffs chances. Here's how CSK can still qualify for the IPL 2026 playoffs.

Marsh rides the Lucknow bounce, puts huge dent in CSK's playoffs chancesIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings' playoffs chances became really complicated after the team lost to Lucknow Super Giants on Friday, May 15. Mitchell Marsh blew away the side with a scintillating innings of 90 runs.

High level ICC team reaches Sri Lanka amid leadership crisis in SLCA high-level ICC delegation, including a senior BCCI official, has reached Sri Lanka following the government's appointment of an interim committee to run Sri Lanka Cricket. The visit comes at a sensitive time.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
